motivo: código extraído mas apenas 5/6 seções presentes
resposta:
```python
import heapq
import bisect

class MKAverage:

    def __init__(self, m: int, k: int):
        self.m = m
        self.k = k
        self.stream = []
        self.min_heap = []
        self.max_heap = []
        self.sorted_stream = []

    def addElement(self, num: int) -> None:
        self.stream.append(num)
        self.sorted_stream.append(num)
        self.sorted_stream.sort()
        heapq.heappush(self.min_heap, num)
        heapq.heappush(self.max_heap, -num)

        if len(self.min_heap) > self.m:
            smallest = heapq.heappop(self.min_heap)
            bisect.insort(self.sorted_stream, smallest)
            self.sorted_stream.pop()
            self.max_heap.append(-smallest)
            heapq.heapify(self.max_heap)

        if len(self.max_heap) > self.m:
            largest = -heapq.heappop(self.max_heap)
            bisect.insort(self.sorted_stream, largest)
            self.sorted_stream.pop()
            self.min_heap.append(largest)
            heapq.heapify(self.min_heap)

    def calculateMKAverage(self) -> int:
        if len(self.stream) < self.m:
            return -1
        return sum(self.sorted_stream[self.k:self.m-self.k]) // (self.m - 2 * self.k)
```

solution: 
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(m log m) for addElement and O(m) for calculateMKAverage
space complexity: O(m)
energy implications: LOW
